Class GameController

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.Game.Core.GameController
public class GameController extends Object
Coordinates incoming commands from the network layer and translates them into operations on the Game model.

This controller acts as the central command processor in the MVC architecture. It is designed to be lightweight, as the high-level game flow is driven by client actions received via the network. It ensures that commands are parsed correctly and executed only when the game state allows (e.g., checking initialization).

It also manages "Thinking Timers" to ensure the game progresses if a player is inactive.

    Description

    Constructs a new GameController bounded to a specific game instance.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameController

      public GameController(Game game)
      Constructs a new GameController bounded to a specific game instance.
      Parameters:
      game - The Game instance to be managed.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided game is null.

    • GameController

      public GameController(Game game, GameResultRepository gameResultRepository)
      Constructs a new GameController bounded to a specific game instance.
      Parameters:
      game - The Game instance to be managed.
      gameResultRepository - repository used to persist end-of-game scores (may be null).
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the provided game is null.

  • Method Details

    • getGame

      public Game getGame()
      Gets the game model instance currently handled by this controller.
      Returns:
      The active Game instance.

    • configureLobby

      public void configureLobby(String creatorNickname, int requiredPlayers)
      Configures room-level lobby constraints received from the network layer.
      Parameters:
      creatorNickname - room creator nickname, currently not used by the ready flow.
      requiredPlayers - number of players requested by the creator.

    • handleCommand

      public String handleCommand(String rawCommand)
      Parses a raw textual command and executes the corresponding game action.

      This method is synchronized to ensure thread-safe access to the Game model when multiple ClientHandler threads attempt to process actions simultaneously. It handles command tokenization, basic validation, and error reporting. Processing a valid command will cancel any active thinking timer.

      Parameters:
      rawCommand - The string command received from a client handler.
      Returns:
      A human-readable feedback message or error string to be returned to the client.

    • handlePlayerCommand

      public String handlePlayerCommand(String nickname, String rawCommand)
      Handles a command coming from a specific logged-in player.

      This method keeps backward compatibility with existing tests by delegating to handleCommand(String) after setting a per-thread nickname context.

      Parameters:
      nickname - acting player's nickname.
      rawCommand - raw command string.
      Returns:
      controller response.

    • startTurnTimer

      public void startTurnTimer(Player player)
      Starts a thinking timer for the specified player.

      If the player does not send a command within THINKING_TIME_LIMIT seconds, the checkAndExecuteAutoMove(Player) method is triggered.

      Parameters:
      player - The player who must make a decision.

    • registerPlayer

      public void registerPlayer(String nickname)
      Registers a new nickname in the game model by assigning it to the first available player slot.
      Parameters:
      nickname - The nickname to register.

    • setPlayerOffline

      public void setPlayerOffline(String nickname)
      Marks a player as offline in the game model.

      This is called by the network layer when a client disconnects or times out. Marking a player offline allows the game to continue using auto-action logic. If the player was currently active, an auto-move is triggered.

      Parameters:
      nickname - The nickname of the player who went offline.

    • statusSnapshot

      public String statusSnapshot()
      Returns a status payload meant for UI/network synchronization. Unlike handleCommand(String) with "status", this always returns the structured snapshot even after the game has finished (so GUI parsers keep working).

    • isGameFinished

      public boolean isGameFinished()
      Returns:
      true once the match has finished and final scoring has been applied.

    • finalReportText

      public String finalReportText()
      Returns:
      final report text if available, otherwise a generic game-over message.
